title: View group members' shared events in group context
body:
## Issue description

With personal event ownership and the new sharing model, group members need a way to see events that other members have shared to the group. Currently, the group event listing (`GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId/events`) shows events created within that group and filters by invitation-based access. The new model requires listing events that have been explicitly shared to the group via `event_shares`, respecting each share's visibility level.

This issue covers the backend logic and API changes for viewing shared events within a group context, as well as the frontend group detail page updates.

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

### 1. Backend — updated group event listing

**Updated `ListGroupEventsUseCase`:**
- Query events shared to the group via `event_shares` table
- For each event, apply the visibility level filter from the share record:
  - `busy` → return minimal data (owner, start/end times only)
  - `title` → return title, start/end times, status, owner
  - `details` → return full event data
- Caller must be a member of the group
- Support date range filtering (`from`, `to` query parameters)
- Support pagination
- Sort by `start_at` by default
- Include the caller's own events shared to this group
- Include a flag or field indicating the visibility level so the client knows how to render

**Response shape per visibility level:**

```json
// busy
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "ownerId": "uuid",
  "ownerDisplayName": "Jane",
  "startAt": "2026-04-01T10:00:00Z",
  "endAt": "2026-04-01T11:00:00Z",
  "visibilityLevel": "busy"
}

// title
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "ownerId": "uuid",
  "ownerDisplayName": "Jane",
  "title": "Team standup",
  "startAt": "2026-04-01T10:00:00Z",
  "endAt": "2026-04-01T11:00:00Z",
  "status": "scheduled",
  "visibilityLevel": "title"
}

// details
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "ownerId": "uuid",
  "ownerDisplayName": "Jane",
  "title": "Team standup",
  "description": "Weekly sync with the team",
  "startAt": "2026-04-01T10:00:00Z",
  "endAt": "2026-04-01T11:00:00Z",
  "status": "scheduled",
  "visibilityLevel": "details",
  "location": { ... }
}
```

### 2. Backend — shared event detail

**Updated or new `GetSharedEventUseCase`:**
- When a group member accesses a shared event, return data according to the share's visibility level
- If `details` level: allow access to event-scoped collaboration (chat, checklist, location) — or gate this separately
- If `busy` or `title` level: do not expose collaboration features
- Verify: caller is a group member AND event is shared to that group

### 3. API changes

**Updated endpoint:**
| Method | Endpoint | Changes |
|--------|----------|----------|
| GET | `/api/v1/groups/:groupId/events` | Returns shared events with visibility-filtered data |
| GET | `/api/v1/groups/:groupId/events/:eventId` | Returns shared event detail respecting visibility level |

**New query parameters for listing:**
- `from` (ISO 8601) — filter events starting on or after this date
- `to` (ISO 8601) — filter events starting before this date
- `page`, `limit` — pagination

### 4. Frontend — group detail page

- Update group detail page to display shared events from the updated API
- Render events differently based on `visibilityLevel`:
  - `busy` → show as opaque block with owner name, no clickable detail
  - `title` → show title and time, limited click-through
  - `details` → show full event card with click-through to event detail
- Handle mixed visibility levels in the same list gracefully

### 5. Privacy enforcement

- Events not shared to the group must not appear in the listing
- Events shared at `busy` level must not expose title or any other detail
- The API must enforce visibility filtering server-side — client rendering is a secondary safeguard only
- Former group members must not see events shared to the group after leaving
- If a share is revoked, the event must disappear from the group view immediately

---

## Additional notes

- Depends on: Database schema migration, Personal event management, Event sharing to groups
- The existing `list-group-events.usecase.ts` needs significant refactoring or replacement
- Consider backward compatibility with legacy group-scoped events during transition
- The `event_invitations` table may still be relevant for RSVP on `details`-level shared events
- Performance: the query joins `event_shares` → `events` → `user_profiles` (for owner name). Ensure proper indexes.
- Follow existing patterns in `event-management` module

### Related files
- `src/modules/event-management/application/list-group-events.usecase.ts`
- `src/modules/event-management/application/get-event.usecase.ts`
- `src/modules/event-management/infrastructure/knex-event.repository.ts`
- `src/modules/event-management/presentation/event.router.ts`
- `client/src/pages/GroupDetail/`
- `client/src/api/events.ts`

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId/events` returns events shared to the group via `event_shares`
- [ ] Response data is filtered per event's `visibilityLevel` (busy, title, details)
- [ ] `busy` events return only owner, start/end times — no title or description
- [ ] `title` events return owner, title, start/end times, status — no description
- [ ] `details` events return full event data
- [ ] Date range filtering works (`from` and `to` query parameters)
- [ ] Pagination works correctly
- [ ] Only group members can access the endpoint
- [ ] Events not shared to the group do not appear
- [ ] Revoked shares cause events to disappear from the group listing
- [ ] Former group members cannot see shared events after leaving
- [ ] Frontend renders events according to visibility level
- [ ] `busy` events display as opaque time blocks without clickable details
- [ ] Unit tests cover visibility-level data filtering logic
- [ ] Integration tests cover shared event queries with different visibility levels
- [ ] API tests verify authorization and visibility enforcement
- [ ] Privacy tests confirm no data leakage beyond the configured visibility level
- [ ] CI pipeline passes
